name: Watermelon Boat
recipeIngredient: 1 large honeydew melon, cut into cubes/balls
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine lemon juice, sugar, and flour in a saucepan; bring to a boil.
2. Reduce heat to low. Stir 1/4 cup of the mixture into eggs; return all to pan.
3. Cook and stir for 15 minutes or until the mixture coats a spoon (do not boil).
4. Cool. Fold in whipped cream; cover and chill until serving.
5. For watermelon boat, cut a thin slice from the bottom of the melon with a sharp knife to allow it to sit flat.
6. Mark a horizontal cutting line 2 inches above the center of the melon.
7. With a long sharp knife, cut into the melon along the cutting line, making sure to cut all the way through.
8. Gently pull off the top section of rind. Remove the fruit from both sections and cut into cubes or balls; set aside.
9. To cut a decorative edge, place the melon on its side.
10. Position an 8-point star cookie cutter against the inside edge of the melon, allowing only half of the star to cut through.
11. Insert a toothpick into the flat edge of the removed piece.
12. Attach the piece onto the melon edge where the last cut ends. Repeat cutting and attaching pieces until the entire melon edge is completed.
13. Combine honeydew, cantaloupe, strawberries, grapes, and watermelon; spoon into the boat.
14. Serve dressing on the side.
Yield: 32-36 servings (about 2 cups dressing).
